text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Insufficient maternal iodine intake is associated with subfecundity, reduced foetal growth, and adverse pregnancy outcomes in the Norwegian Mother, Father and Child Cohort Study.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Insufficient maternal iodine intake is associated with subfecundity, reduced foetal growth, and adverse pregnancy outcomes in the Norwegian Mother, Father and Child Cohort Study.","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states the observed association between low maternal iodine intake and the listed adverse reproductive outcomes, supporting the claim.","population":"Norwegian Mother, Father and Child Cohort Study participants","intervention":"Insufficient maternal iodine intake","comparator":"","outcome":"subfecundity, reduced foetal growth, adverse pregnancy outcomes","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"insufficient","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Suboptimal maternal iodine intake is associated with impaired child neurodevelopment at 3 years of age in the Norwegian Mother and Child Cohort Study.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Suboptimal Maternal Iodine Intake Is Associated with Impaired Child Neurodevelopment at 3 Years of Age in the Norwegian Mother and Child Cohort Study.","entailment":"The quoted sentence reports an observed link between low maternal iodine intake and poorer neurodevelopmental outcomes in children at age three, providing evidence for the claim.","population":"Norwegian Mother and Child Cohort Study children at 3 years","intervention":"Suboptimal maternal iodine intake","comparator":"","outcome":"impaired child neurodevelopment","timeframe":"3 years of age","quantifiers":"suboptimal","modality":"associational"}]}
